query: I definitely don’t want to change my plan, but I’m willing to refuel 2GB of data. Please go ahead and add 2GB so I can get back to excellent internet speed.

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: Reports that mobile data is stopping or running very slowly and wants excellent internet speed.
assistant: Verified the account by phone number and identified the affected line 555-123-2002.
assistant: Checked usage and found the line had exceeded its plan data limit.
assistant: Explained that a plan change was optional but a 2 GB data refuel was available immediately to restore service and asked whether the user wanted to proceed.
user: Declined a plan change and explicitly authorized a 2 GB refuel.

candidate_answers.text: Understood — I’ll add the 2 GB refuel now so you can get your mobile data working again without changing your plan.
The user has explicitly chosen the offered 2 GB top-up as the remedy for exhausted data, so the grounded next step is to perform the refuel instead of reopening plan discussions.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a user authorization turn where the exceeded-data diagnosis has already been surfaced and the user explicitly accepts the offered 2 GB remedy.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the visible state. Scores reward immediate execution of the approved refuel and suppress alternative troubleshooting or escalation actions that ignore the freshly authorized fix.
notes: This sample provides a telecom action type not present in 510 by labeling a direct commercial remediation step rather than escalation.
The visible state is sufficient because the user is responding to a previously offered, grounded 2 GB refuel option.
